problem_addressed: Large language models often produce suboptimal outputs on their first attempt, especially for tasks with complex requirements or poorly defined objectives.
core_claim: SELF-REFINE can significantly improve LLM outputs through iterative self-feedback without requiring additional training, data, or reinforcement learning.
key_contributions: A novel approach for iterative refinement using self-feedback within a single LLM
Demonstrated effectiveness across 7 diverse tasks with ~20% absolute improvement in task performance
novelty: Unlike prior approaches that require training data, refinement models, or reinforcement learning, SELF-REFINE uses a single LLM as the generator, refiner, and feedback provider.
method_summary: SELF-REFINE generates an initial output using an LLM, then has the same LLM provide feedback for its output and use it to refine itself iteratively.
key_innovation: Using a single LLM to both generate feedback and refine its own outputs without any additional training or data.
assumptions: The LLM can provide useful feedback for its own outputs
Iterative refinement will lead to improvements in output quality
hyperparameters: T=0.0 and T=0.7 for decoding, N=5 iterations, InstructGPT model (text-davinci-003)
headline_result: GPT-4 with SELF-REFINE on Constrained Generation: 45.0% (from 15.0% base)
ablations: Error analysis shows model robustness to bad feedback (60%) and issues like ignoring feedback (25%)
improvement_over_baseline: Varies by task; e.g., GPT-4 Constrained Gen. improved by 30.0 percentage points
best_result: GPT-4 with SELF-REFINE on Constrained Generation: 45.0% (from 15.0% base)
title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback
